ongoing_stories.headline: China nationwide AV permit freeze
ongoing_stories.last_status: Active — no resumption timeline confirmed as of June 1, 2026. MIIT suspended all new AV permits following Apollo Go system-wide outage. Freeze blocks fleet additions, new test programmes, and new city expansions. Existing services continue to operate.
ongoing_stories.conflicting_data: Outage trigger date disputed — March 31, 2026 (EVWire/DIGITIMES, localized Wuhan incident, Score: 2) vs April 27, 2026 (Bloomberg/Automotive World, system-wide recurrence, Score: 3). Working conclusion: April 27 system-wide event most likely triggered the April 29 MIIT freeze; March 31 was a precursor. LOW PRIORITY — Human verification recommended against Chinese-language official sources.
status_change_triggers: Official MIIT or Ministry of Transport announcement on resumption or extension
Any operator receiving a new AV permit
Any official timeline given or revised
Any additional operator sanctioned or penalized
Official Baidu statement confirming or clarifying the disputed outage trigger date
Any regulatory document citing a specific confirmed date

ongoing_stories.headline: Apollo Go outage trigger date — March 31 vs April 27 conflict
ongoing_stories.last_status: Unresolved — Baidu has not publicly disclosed root cause or confirmed the triggering date. Two credible source groups cite different dates. No official clarification issued.
ongoing_stories.conflicting_data: March 31 cited by EVWire/DIGITIMES (Score: 2 — Tier 3, single corroboration, specific date). April 27 cited by Bloomberg/Automotive World (Score: 3 — Tier 3, two corroborations, more recent reporting). Working conclusion: April 27 system-wide event is the more likely MIIT trigger. LOW PRIORITY — Human verification recommended.
status_change_triggers: Official Baidu statement confirming or clarifying the date
Regulatory document from MIIT citing a specific date
Third-party independent investigation finding published by credible source

ongoing_stories.headline: Waymo NHTSA recall — 3,791 vehicles, flooding defect
ongoing_stories.last_status: Open — interim OTA fix deployed (tighter weather restrictions, updated map data) but no permanent fix submitted to NHTSA. NHTSA has flagged in writing that no permanent fix has been received. San Antonio resumption expected imminently pending final remedy.
status_change_triggers: Permanent fix submitted to or approved by NHTSA
Recall scope expanded or narrowed
NHTSA closes or escalates the investigation
Waymo issues a new interim fix or OTA update
San Antonio or Atlanta operations formally resumed

ongoing_stories.headline: Waymo freeway ride suspension — construction zone edge cases
ongoing_stories.last_status: Ongoing — surface streets operational in SF, LA, Phoenix, Miami. All freeway rides suspended after viral video of vehicle navigating construction cones. Company integrating technical learnings; expects resumption 'soon' — no date given. Root cause not publicly disclosed.
status_change_triggers: Official freeway resumption announced by Waymo
Suspension extended to additional cities
Root cause publicly disclosed
Software fix confirmed deployed via OTA
Atlanta operations formally resumed

ongoing_stories.headline: Apollo Go Wuhan service shutdown — ongoing post-outage
ongoing_stories.last_status: Ongoing — Apollo Go service in Wuhan remains shut down following the mass vehicle immobilization incident. No resumption date announced. All other Apollo Go cities continue operating under existing permits.
ongoing_stories.conflicting_data: See apollo-go-outage-date-conflict entry for disputed dates of the triggering event.
status_change_triggers: Official Apollo Go or Baidu announcement of Wuhan service resumption
Regulatory approval granted for Wuhan operations to restart
Root cause of outage publicly disclosed by Baidu
